scenarios.opening_query: My wife and I are done, but our biggest asset is my pension that doesn't pay out for nine more years.
scenarios.hidden_fact: We've settled everything else by agreement — drafting the pension division order correctly, with survivor elections and the nine-year horizon, IS the remaining case.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: Everything else is settled — the pension division order, survivor elections and all, is the remaining case.
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: Nothing's settled — she disputes the pension's marital share and the whole dissolution is contested.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Nothing's settled — even the pension's marital share is contested.

scenarios.opening_query: I found divorce papers in my husband's desk drawer, unfiled, dated three months ago.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Family Law > General (Divorce/Separation etc.) || Family Law > Document Review
scenarios.hidden_fact: Tucked with them is a signed 'marital settlement agreement' he apparently wants me to sign — before anything else I need someone to review what those drafted terms actually give away.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: There's a drafted settlement agreement with them he wants signed — I need those terms reviewed before anything.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether drafted terms need review first
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No agreement — just the petition; I want my own counsel and my own filing first.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Just the petition — I want my own counsel and to file first.

scenarios.opening_query: My husband's ex keeps dragging him back to court and it's draining our family's savings.
scenarios.hidden_fact: The 'dragging' is all support-modification motions — his income varies as a fisherman, and a properly structured variable-income support order would end the annual warfare.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's all support modifications — his fishing income varies, and a proper variable-income order would end the annual warfare.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a structural support-order fix is the need
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's property relitigating — she keeps attacking the decree's asset division with new theories, and finality motions are the need.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's the property division she keeps attacking — finality motions are the need.

scenarios.opening_query: We adopted my stepson years ago and now in the divorce my husband claims he owes 'nothing for another man's kid.'
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Family Law > General (Divorce/Separation etc.) || Family Law > Child Support/Modification
scenarios.hidden_fact: The adoption decree makes him a legal parent, full stop — establishing the support obligation on those grounds is the concrete fight inside the divorce.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The adoption decree makes him a legal parent, full stop — establishing support on that basis is the concrete fight.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether an adoption decree settles the parentage question
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The adoption was never finalized — the paperwork stalled years ago, which reopens parentage questions inside the dissolution itself.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The adoption never finalized — the stalled paperwork reopens parentage inside the divorce.

scenarios.opening_query: After thirty years my parents are divorcing and my mother has never once paid a bill or seen a statement.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Family Law > General (Divorce/Separation etc.) || Wills & Trusts > Power of Attorney
scenarios.hidden_fact: Mom's early dementia complicates everything — she may lack capacity to instruct divorce counsel, and whether I need protective authority for her comes before the divorce can even proceed.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: Mom has early dementia — capacity to instruct counsel is in question, so protective authority for her may come before the divorce can proceed.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether capacity questions gate the dissolution
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: Mom's sharp — just financially inexperienced; a strong divorce team with forensic accounting is the whole need.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: She's sharp, just inexperienced — a strong divorce team with forensic accounting covers it.

scenarios.opening_query: My husband served me papers, and his family's lawyer 'generously' offered to handle everything for both of us.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Family Law > Document Review || Family Law > General (Divorce/Separation etc.)
scenarios.hidden_fact: I've realized the 'shared lawyer' represents HIM — the proposed terms give him the house and my retirement; I need my own full representation, not a document check.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: That 'shared lawyer' represents him — the terms give him the house and my retirement. I need my own full representation.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether conflicted counsel makes full representation necessary
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The terms are genuinely fair — we built them together; I just want independent eyes on the final draft before signing.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The terms are fair and we built them together — I just want independent eyes before signing.
